title: Animal-based vs. keto with Taylor Rapp

chunks.title: Eating Habits and Carbohydrate Sources
chunks.summary: The speakers discuss their current eating habits, focusing on carbohydrate sources. One mentions getting three-quarters of their carbs from fruit and honey, while the other notes a preference for sweet potatoes and avoids gluten.

chunks.title: Impact of Diet on Physical Performance
chunks.summary: The speakers explore how following an animal-based diet has improved their physical performance and range of motion. They note a significant reduction in inflammation and better recovery times since adopting the diet.
chunks.transcript: most players in the league because I talked to a couple other NFL players they're getting carbs from like oatmeal like pancakes you don't do that I don't do that that's great I I don't I don't think I do well with like gluten yeah and like what happens I just I feel inflamed bloated your joints hurt a little more joints hurt yeah a little creepier like if like say I I don't know I I like ever since I've been animal based like I've been you you like do back spot or stuff like that I do I mean I I'm Surfer so but I know what you mean okay so like I don't do it as much so like I'm I'm really big into like uh depth deep as hell right like in full range of motion and like ever since I like made this transformation go more animal based and like cut out like all the inflammatory uh oils right and uh grains all that type of stuff um like I've been able to get more range of motion deeper deeper squats like sometimes like I I swear like when I was in college like I would try to go deep and like I feel like my my hips were like you know like like an old man like creaky you know I wasn't able to as smooth or like I don't know I think there's something there you know and ever since I started that um you know started the animal based diet I feel like I've I've been able to go more and range of motion deeper squats uh better range of motion all that that's amazing now you had a period

chunks.title: Experience with Keto Diet
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to a past experience with the keto diet during college. They reflect on the negative impacts it had on their recovery and overall well-being, such as cramping and ineffective muscle recovery.

chunks.title: Current Carbohydrate Intake
chunks.summary: The speakers discuss their current carbohydrate intake, comparing amounts consumed and sharing personal experiences with energy levels. They highlight feeling better with increased carbohydrate intake and the impact it has on their recovery.

chunks.title: Blood Glucose Monitoring and Health
chunks.summary: They delve into the use of continuous glucose monitors, expressing concerns about how they can create fear surrounding blood sugar spikes. The speakers argue that not all spikes are harmful, especially in healthy individuals.

chunks.title: Carbohydrates and Hormonal Health
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the broader implications of carbohydrate consumption on hormonal health, particularly regarding fertility and menstrual cycles in women. They emphasize the need for adequate carbohydrates for optimal bodily functions.
